message:
refactor(sync): rename_jsonl_temp_output takes &Path, not PathBuf

Tiny follow-up to f668b14. rename_jsonl_temp_output doesn't need
ownership of temp_path — it reads it for
require_safe_sync_overwrite_path and crate::util::durable_rename,
and temp_guard (which DID need ownership so .persist() could
consume it) already handles the cleanup-or-commit side. Drop the
signature to `&Path`, remove the `mut` on temp_guard in the
persist_jsonl_temp_output destructure (no longer moved into
rename_jsonl_temp_output), and update both call sites to pass
`&temp_path`.

No behavioural change. Matches the reference-passing pattern used
by every other helper on the sync path that doesn't need to
consume the path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
